title: narcissus assoanus subsp. praelongus
intro: Narcissus assoanus subsp. praelongus, also known as the Narcissus pseudonarcissus, is a subspecies of the daffodil plant, specifically a type of wild daffodil native to the Iberian Peninsula and parts of France. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which make it useful in treating various health issues such as colds, fever, and respiratory problems. The health benefits of Narcissus assoanus subsp. praelongus include its potential to lower blood pressure, reduce inflammation, and act as an expectorant to relieve congestion and coughs. The plant is typically used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, and its bulbs are sometimes dried and used as a natural remedy.
